texto: I don't know what happened, but I do know this. It's not going anywhere. When you light up it waits for you to come down. You have to confront whatever's bothering you and look it straight in the eye. It's alright to forgive yourself, and it's okay to fight back, because if you don't kick the shit out of it, then it kicks you. It's a dog world, but you can control it, if you want to. A lot of people are going to try to make you feel like shit, but that doesn't mean you are. You are who you decide to be. I hope you're the kind of person that fights, because that's the only way to win.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a E.M. Youman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I don't know what happened, but I do know this. It's not going anywhere. When you light up it wai…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, addiction, addiction-and-recovery pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e addiction-and-recovery.

You have to confront whatever's bothering you and look it straight in the eye. It's alright to forgive yourself, and it's okay to fight back, because if you don't kick the shit out of it, then it kicks you. It's a dog world, but you can control it, if you want to. texto: No one is entitled to anything. Everything we get in this life we have worked for. And sometimes we take on baggage we never even signed up for, but that dosen't mean you deserve it. I wake up everyday wishing I could change things, but I can't change past. All I can do is change the future.
explicacao: Citação de E.M. Youman sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “No one is entitled to anything. Everything we get in this life we have worked for. And sometimes …” articula reflexao, addiction, addiction-and-recovery em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e trabalho.

frase_original: No one is entitled to anything. Everything we get in this life we have worked for. And sometimes we take on baggage we never even signed up for, but that dosen't mean you deserve it. I wake up everyday wishing I could change things, but I can't change past. All I can do is change the future.

texto: She looked into his eyes, hoping he could give her something to hang on to. His response saddened her. "No. I wish I could give you a better answer, but I don't have it all planned out. I just try to be best I can today. Things like being happy, having a good time, have different definitions for me than they used to. Before you start trying to have a good time or figure out where you're going, first find out who you are. If you don't like yourself, that's the starting point, not the end point.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a E. M. Youman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “She looked into his eyes, hoping he could give her something to hang on to. His response saddened…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, dark-times, drug-abuse pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e termino.

frase_original: She looked into his eyes, hoping he could give her something to hang on to. His response saddened her. "No. I wish I could give you a better answer, but I don't have it all planned out. I just try to be best I can today. Things like being happy, having a good time, have different definitions for me than they used to. Before you start trying to have a good time or figure out where you're going, first find out who you are. If you don't like yourself, that's the starting point, not the end point.
